label: .env.local sets SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true; launcher loads it before spawning child
evidence: .env.local:4 — SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true (gitignored)
.env.local:1-3 — comment documents this is maintainer-only; forces INDEX_* flags

label: Launcher forces all 5 INDEX_* flags to 'true' when MAINTAINER_MODE=true is in process.env
evidence: cjs:65-71 — loadEnvFile loop loads .env.local before any child spawn
cjs:80 — if (process.env.SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE === 'true')
cjs:81-87 — INDEX_KEYS array: SKILLS, AGENTS, COMMANDS, SPECS, PLUGINS
cjs:88-90 — for loop sets each to 'true'
cjs:52-55 — loadEnvFile only sets if !(key in process.env); .env.local wins over .env but existing process.env wins over file values

label: Neither buildChildEnv (cjs) nor buildDaemonEnv (mjs) scrubs SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE — it propagates to child daemon
evidence: cjs:21 — BLOCKED_CHILD_ENV_RE = /^(NODE_|npm_|NPM_)/
cjs:645-652 — buildChildEnv copies all process.env EXCEPT blocked prefixes; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE is NOT blocked
mjs:49 — DAEMON_ENV_DENYLIST only blocks credential/token patterns (GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS_, etc.)
mjs:57-64 — buildDaemonEnv copies all process.env EXCEPT undefined values and denylist matches; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE is NOT blocked
Both code paths propagate MAINTAINER_MODE to the child daemon process

label: Interactive session: child exits at cjs:864 before scan — leak untriggered; clean env: child reaches cjs:944 with INDEX_* forced — leak fires
evidence: cjs:850-854 — acquireOwnerLeaseFile returns acquired:false when live owner holds lease
cjs:864 — return; exits child BEFORE buildIfNeeded at cjs:944
cjs:944 — buildIfNeeded is where forced INDEX_* scan would trigger
Q4 established: in interactive sessions child exits at cjs:864 — MAINTAINER_MODE has no effect

Clean env: no operator daemon -> acquireOwnerLeaseFile returns acquired:true -> cjs:944 reached -> forced INDEX_* scan fires with all flags true -> graph-metadata.json rewritten across .opencode/{skills,specs,agents,commands,plugins}

label: skip-not-fail fix is ORTHOGONAL to maintainer-mode leak — they address different failure modes
evidence: skip-not-fail: mjs:397-404 — converts live-owner contention from FAIL to SKIP when harness child cannot acquire lease
Maintainer-mode leak: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true forces unintended full INDEX scan in clean env
The two are independent: skip-not-fail does not prevent the maintainer-mode scan from firing when the child DOES acquire the lease (clean env)
Fixing skip-not-fail does NOT fix the maintainer-mode env leak

label: Verdict: maintainer-mode leak is HIDDEN (not fixed) — interactive sessions untrigger it; clean env triggers it
evidence: The env var is NEVER scrubbed: it propagates from .env.local -> launcher process -> child daemon
Interactive session: child exits at cjs:864 BEFORE the env var can cause any effect — leak is UNTRIGGERED, not neutralized
Clean env (no operator daemon): child acquires lease, reaches cjs:944, INDEX_*='true' forces full scan — leak is LIVE
skip-not-fail is orthogonal: it does not prevent the clean-env scan path
Conclusion: the leak is MERELY HIDDEN in interactive sessions, not fixed

label: Clean-env blast radius: forced INDEX_* scan with all 5 flags true rewrites graph-metadata.json across .opencode/{skills,agents,commands,specs,plugins}
evidence: cjs:81-87 — all 5 INDEX_* flags forced to 'true'

Scan targets: .opencode/skills/*/ (all skill packages), .opencode/agents/*/ (all agent definitions), .opencode/commands/*/ (all commands), .opencode/specs/*/ (all packet spec folders), .opencode/plugins/*/ (all plugins)
Each directory gets a graph-metadata.json written during forced scan
Clean env = no operator daemon running (CI, fresh environment) — the harness child IS the scanning entity
The ~1437 graph-metadata.json modifications are attributed to operator's own daemon rescans; clean env harness child would produce the same effect if .env.local is present

direction: skip-not-fail fix addresses the maintainer-mode env leak
reason: skip-not-fail converts live-owner contention FAIL->SKIP; maintainer-mode leak fires in CLEAN ENV where there is no live owner to contend with — different code path, different failure mode, orthogonal fix

DAEMON_ENV_DENYLIST only blocks credential/token patterns (GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS_, etc.); SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE is not in the denylist; mjs:60-61 shows it passes through to the child daemon env
BLOCKED_CHILD_ENV_RE at cjs:21 only blocks NODE_/npm_/NPM_ prefixes; SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE is not blocked; cjs:648 shows it passes through to the child process

direction: interactive session leak is 'sidestepped' (env var neutralized)
reason: The env var is NEVER neutralized — it propagates to the child daemon. The leak is UNTRIGGERED because the child exits at cjs:864 before reaching the scan. 'Sidestepped' implies the fix prevents the leak; 'hidden' means the leak exists but the conditions to trigger it are not met. This is HIDDEN.

question: Is the SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true leak genuinely sidestepped by skip-not-fail, or merely hidden?
answer: MERELY HIDDEN. The leak is real and unneutralized: SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_MAINTAINER_MODE=true in .env.local is loaded by the launcher (cjs:65-71), forces all 5 INDEX_* flags (cjs:80-94), and propagates to the child daemon via buildChildEnv (cjs:645) which does NOT block it. In interactive sessions, the child exits at cjs:864 before buildIfNeeded at cjs:944, so the leak never fires — but it is UNTRIGGERED, not FIXED. In clean env (no operator daemon, e.g. CI), the child acquires the lease, reaches cjs:944, and the forced INDEX_* scan fires, rewriting graph-metadata.json across .opencode/{skills,specs,agents,commands,plugins}. skip-not-fail is orthogonal: it handles live-owner contention (FAIL->SKIP), not the maintainer-mode scan in clean env. The ~1437 graph-metadata.json files are pre-existing + operator daemon rescans; a clean-env harness run with .env.local present would produce the same blast radius.
